trends.name: Korean Ssam Bar Snacks
trends.short descriptor: Lettuce, ssamjang, sizzling picks — the table refuses to quiet down.
trends.WHY IT'S HITTING:
Built for grazing and group ordering.
Every bite feels customizable.
Lettuce wraps make rich food feel lighter.
Big flavor without full KBBQ commitment.
WHAT TO ORDER: Pork belly ssam with perilla leaf, rice, and ssamjang — build it yourself, eat it in one bite
trends.MOST SPOTTED:
Jilli (Koreatown)
Soban (Koreatown)
Mapo Dak Galbi (Koreatown)
Yangmani (Koreatown)
trends.WORTH THE SPLURGE: Soban (Koreatown) — deeper banchan range and more composed shared-table spread
trends.EASY ENTRY: Yangmani (Koreatown) — energetic, flavor-forward Korean plates that stay easy for groups
trends.moveCopy: Wrap it, drag it through sauce, repeat.

reasons: Limited named editorial authority in source list — treat as provisional

Google Places / geo evidence supports venue-level concentration

whyItsEverywhereNarrative.headlineReason: Signal is mostly non-editorial or manual — narrative should stay cautious until publications align.

publicNarrative.primaryLine: We’re catching small echoes around Korean Ssam Bar Snacks—think Koreatown and Arts District—enough to note, not enough to call a scene.
supportingLines: Restaurant lines and neighborhood overlaps keep repeating the same flavor shorthand.

trends.evidenceSummary: Google Places (weekend): 20 result(s) for “Korean Ssam Bar Snacks” (query “Korean Ssam Bar Snacks Los Angeles”) · avg rating 4.5 · ~8251 review rollups. Open listings (aggregated): 400 open listings across 8 mapped search slices blended avg ★4.3 · ~195040 review volume indexed. “Korean Ssam Bar Snacks”: 50 open (★3.7); “Aguachile on Ice”: 50 open (★4.5); “Korean food”: 50 open (★4.4); “Olive Oil Dessert Loops”: 50 open (★4.5).

trends.name: Sonoran-Style Breakfast Burritos
trends.short descriptor: Thin flour tortillas, machaca, eggs, potatoes, and griddled edges.
trends.WHY IT'S HITTING:
Handmade flour tortillas are becoming the star.
Machaca adds deeper savory texture than bacon.
Regional Mexican breakfast styles are replacing generic burritos.
Crisp griddled edges create texture without heaviness.
WHAT TO ORDER: Machaca breakfast burrito on a thin flour tortilla
Potato-and-egg burrito with crisped griddled edges
Northern Mexico-style handmade flour tortilla breakfast wrap
trends.MOST SPOTTED:
Macheen (Boyle Heights)
Tacos Villa Corona (Atwater Village)
Cilantro Mexican Grill (Glassell Park)
Burritos La Palma (El Monte)
trends.WORTH THE SPLURGE: Macheen (Boyle Heights) — Sonoran flour-tortilla structure, machaca depth, and crisp griddled finish
trends.EASY ENTRY: Tacos Villa Corona (Atwater Village) — straightforward potato-egg burrito format with true morning pace

trends.name: Hainan Chicken Lunch Counters
trends.short descriptor: Poached bird, schmaltz-y rice, sauce trio — counter-speed SGV lunch.
trends.WHY IT'S HITTING:
The chicken is quiet; the rice does the talking.
It feels clean, but still fully lunch.
Sauces turn one plate into three moods.
WHAT TO ORDER: Hainan chicken rice — poached bird, chicken-fat rice, ginger-scallion oil
Three-sauce tray — chile, ginger-scallion, sweet dark soy
Extra chicken rice side — lunch-counter refill culture
trends.MOST SPOTTED: Savoy Kitchen (Alhambra) — Hainan chicken rice — poached bird, chicken-fat rice, ginger-scallion oil
trends.WORTH THE SPLURGE: Pearl River Deli (Chinatown) — Extra chicken rice side — lunch-counter refill culture
trends.EASY ENTRY: Savoy Kitchen (Alhambra) — Hainan chicken rice — poached bird, chicken-fat rice, ginger-scallion oil

trends.name: Aguachile on Ice
trends.short descriptor: Shrimp in an electric lime-chile bath — colder than your beer, louder than small talk.
trends.WHY IT'S HITTING:
It hits before the table even settles.
Citrus, chile, ice — LA's holy trinity.
Light enough to keep the night moving.
WHAT TO ORDER: Shrimp aguachile verde — heavy lime, serrano heat, sliced cucumber, served ice-cold with tostadas.
trends.MOST SPOTTED: Holbox (Downtown LA) — Shrimp aguachile verde — heavy lime, serrano heat, sliced cucumber, served ice-cold with tostadas.
trends.WORTH THE SPLURGE: Holbox — pristine seafood, deeper citrus, cleaner heat.
trends.EASY ENTRY: Mariscos Jalisco — faster, louder, and just as craveable.
trends.WHY IT WORKS: Cold seafood, high acid, and chile heat — it wakes up the whole table.
trends.moveCopy: Order it cold, eat it first.

trends.name: Natural Wine “Martini” Hour
trends.short descriptor: Orange wine in a stem, martini brain, golden hour spilling onto the sidewalk.
trends.WHY IT'S HITTING:
It scratches the cocktail itch without going full cocktail.
Salty, cold, bitter — very pre-dinner.
Makes 6 p.m. feel like a plan.
WHAT TO ORDER: Skin-contact white or chilled red — served cold, slightly cloudy, poured fast and refilled often
Skin-contact white or chilled red — served cold, slightly cloudy, poured fast and refilled often
Martini or spritz round — aperitivo pacing with funky wine still in rotation
trends.MOST SPOTTED: Bar Bandini (Echo Park); Tabula Rasa (Silver Lake); Donna's (Echo Park)
trends.WORTH THE SPLURGE: Bar Bandini (Echo Park) — deeper list, more interesting pours, slower vibe
trends.EASY ENTRY: Tabula Rasa (Silver Lake) — approachable, louder, easy to slide into

aboutToHit.name: Olive Oil Dessert Loops
aboutToHit.short descriptor: Whipped mascarpone, citrus, and grassy olive oil looping onto cakes and soft-serve on dessert-only cards.
aboutToHit.WHY IT'S HITTING: Guests still want ‘clean’ finales. Olive oil reads luxury without chocolate heaviness—easy to test as a special.
WHAT TO ORDER: Olive oil soft serve swirl
Semolina cake + bergamot
Whipped ricotta olive oil dip dessert
aboutToHit.MOST SPOTTED: Botanica Restaurant (Silver Lake) — Olive oil soft serve swirl
aboutToHit.WORTH THE SPLURGE: République Café (Mid-Wilshire) — Whipped ricotta olive oil dip dessert
aboutToHit.EASY ENTRY: Botanica Restaurant (Silver Lake) — Olive oil soft serve swirl

aboutToHit.name: Filipino BBQ Nights
aboutToHit.short descriptor: Calamansi, smoke, vinegar, and charcoal-grilled skewers.
aboutToHit.WHY IT'S HITTING:
Skewer culture turns dinner into an activity.
Smoke and vinegar keep rich flavors balanced.
Filipino BBQ is becoming LA's next great late-night food scene.
Charcoal grilling creates instant visual craving.
WHAT TO ORDER: Charcoal-grilled Filipino pork skewers with banana ketchup and vinegar dips
Calamansi-forward BBQ sticks served with sawsawan and garlic rice
Late-night mixed skewer set with smoke-heavy grill flavor
aboutToHit.MOST SPOTTED: Dollar Hits (West Covina) — Charcoal-grilled Filipino pork skewers with banana ketchup and vinegar dips
aboutToHit.WORTH THE SPLURGE: Neri's Restaurant (Walnut) — Charcoal-grilled Filipino pork skewers with banana ketchup and vinegar dips
aboutToHit.EASY ENTRY: Dollar Hits (West Covina) — Charcoal-grilled Filipino pork skewers with banana ketchup and vinegar dips
aboutToHit.moveCopy: Point at the skewers and keep going.

aboutToHit.name: Ube Cheesecake
aboutToHit.short descriptor: Creamy purple yam cheesecake slices showing up across Filipino bakeries and modern dessert spots.
aboutToHit.WHY IT'S HITTING:
Handmade ube desserts moved from neighborhood staple to citywide craving.
Creamy, dense slices feel nostalgic and modern at the same time.
Bakery cases are leaning hard into vivid purple texture shots.
Filipino pastry flavors are now part of LA's mainstream dessert conversation.
WHAT TO ORDER: Ube cheesecake slice with creamy center and graham-style crust
Burnt-edge basque-style ube cheesecake
Classic bakery ube cheesecake wedge
aboutToHit.MOST SPOTTED: Cafe 86 (Artesia) — Ube cheesecake slice with creamy center and graham-style crust
aboutToHit.WORTH THE SPLURGE: Gemmae Bake Shop (Carson) — Classic bakery ube cheesecake wedge
aboutToHit.EASY ENTRY: Cafe 86 (Artesia) — Ube cheesecake slice with creamy center and graham-style crust
aboutToHit.WHY IT WORKS: Burnt-edge Basque riffs, classic bakery slices, and ube-forward desserts are everywhere right now.
aboutToHit.cuisineOrigin: Modern Filipino dessert culture / bakery trend
aboutToHit.moveCopy: Burnt-edge Basque riffs, classic bakery slices, and ube-forward desserts are everywhere right now.
